description: Wave-optics light-field volume reconstruction of a defocused line-pair target using 3D Richardson-Lucy deconvolution, with thickness-1 benchmark cases and a five-slice volume demo.

microscope.comment: Compact plenoptic-1 fluorescence microscope matching the synthetic wave-model benchmark.

mla.comment: Regular square microlens array. The effective crop used in the task is enlarged by usaf_data.fieldOfViewScale.

reconstruction.comment: Wave-model sampling parameters. The benchmark cases use singleton depth grids so that each observation corresponds to a thickness-1 volume aligned with the sample depth. Solver parameters (RL iterations, theta samples, kernel tolerance) are hardcoded in main.py.

usaf_data.comment: Planar line-pair validation derived from the USAF target experiment in Broxton et al. The physical inverse problem remains 3D volume reconstruction; the benchmark simply occupies one slice of the volume per case.

volume_demo.comment: Generalized volume-reconstruction demonstration. One planar target is placed at -50 um and reconstructed on the five-slice depth grid [-100, -75, -50, -25, 0] um.

reference.paper: M. Broxton, L. Grosenick, S. Yang, N. Cohen, A. Andalman, K. Deisseroth, M. Levoy, Wave optics theory and 3-D deconvolution for the light field microscope, Optics Express 21(21):25418, 2013
reference.local_pdf: Wave optics theory and 3-D deconvolution for t.pdf
reference.background_reference: M. Levoy, Z. Zhang, I. McDowall, Recording and controlling the 4D light field in a microscope using microlens arrays, Journal of Microscopy 235(2):144-162, 2009
reference.implementation_reference_note: See reference_website_github.md for the external wave-optics light-field implementation lineage used to build this cleaned benchmark.
